passage_title: How baby talk gives infant brains a boost

sentences.sentence: The typical way of talking to a baby—high-pitched, exaggerated and repetitious—is a source of fascination for linguists who hope to understand how 'baby talk' impacts on learning.
sentences.translation: 对婴儿说话的典型方式——音调高、夸张且重复——让语言学家着迷，他们希望理解“婴儿语”如何影响学习。

structure.text: The typical way of talking to a baby
high-pitched, exaggerated and repetitious
is a source of fascination for linguists
who hope to understand how 'baby talk' impacts on learning
how 'baby talk' impacts on learning

difficult_points: 两个破折号之间的 high-pitched, exaggerated and repetitious 是插入说明，解释对婴儿说话的典型特征。
who 引导定语从句修饰 linguists，说明语言学家感兴趣的原因。
how 'baby talk' impacts on learning 是名词性从句，作 understand 的宾语。
impacts on learning 表示对学习产生影响，而不是物理撞击。

sentences.sentence: Most babies start developing their hearing while still in the womb, prompting some hopeful parents to play classical music to their pregnant bellies.
sentences.translation: 大多数婴儿还在子宫里时就开始发展听力，这促使一些满怀期待的父母给怀孕的肚子播放古典音乐。

structure.text: Most babies
start developing their hearing
while still in the womb
prompting some hopeful parents to play classical music to their pregnant bellies

difficult_points: while still in the womb 是省略结构，相当于 while they are still in the womb。
prompting ... 是现在分词短语，表示前面事实带来的结果。
to play classical music to their pregnant bellies 是不定式短语，说明父母被促使去做的事。

sentences.sentence: Some research even suggests that infants are listening to adult speech as early as 10 weeks before being born, gathering the basic building blocks of their family's native tongue.
sentences.translation: 一些研究甚至表明，婴儿早在出生前 10 周就在听成人讲话，并在收集其家庭母语的基本构成材料。

structure.text: Some research even suggests
that infants are listening to adult speech as early as 10 weeks before being born
as early as 10 weeks before being born
gathering the basic building blocks of their family's native tongue

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，说明研究提示的内容。
as early as 强调时间非常早，早到出生前 10 周。
being born 是动名词的被动形式，作 before 的宾语。
gathering ... 是现在分词短语，补充说明婴儿听成人语言时获得的信息。

sentences.sentence: Early language exposure seems to have benefits to the brain—for instance, studies suggest that babies raised in bilingual homes are better at learning how to mentally prioritize information.
sentences.translation: 早期语言接触似乎对大脑有益——例如，研究表明，在双语家庭中长大的婴儿更善于学习如何在心理上优先处理信息。

structure.text: Early language exposure
seems to have benefits to the brain
for instance
studies suggest that babies raised in bilingual homes are better at learning how to mentally prioritize information
how to mentally prioritize information

difficult_points: seems to 表示一种研究显示的倾向，而不是绝对断言。
破折号后的 for instance 引出具体研究例证。
raised in bilingual homes 是过去分词短语，修饰 babies。
how to mentally prioritize information 是“疑问词 + 不定式”结构，作 learning 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: So how does the sweet if sometimes absurd sound of infant-directed speech influence a baby's development?
sentences.translation: 那么，婴儿导向语那种甜美但有时荒诞的声音，是如何影响婴儿发展的呢？

structure.text: So
how does the sweet if sometimes absurd sound of infant-directed speech influence a baby's development
the sweet if sometimes absurd sound of infant-directed speech
influence a baby's development

difficult_points: So 承接前一句早期语言接触有益，提出本文核心问题。
if sometimes absurd 是插入的让步表达，表示这种声音虽甜美但有时显得荒诞。
infant-directed speech 指面向婴儿说的话，即 baby talk。
how does ... influence ... 是特殊疑问句结构。

sentences.sentence: Here are some recent studies that explore the science behind baby talk.
sentences.translation: 下面是一些探索婴儿语背后科学原理的近期研究。

structure.text: Here are some recent studies
that explore the science behind baby talk

difficult_points: Here are 用来引出下文列举的研究。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 some recent studies。
the science behind baby talk 指 baby talk 现象背后的科学机制。

sentences.sentence: Fathers don't use baby talk as often or in the same ways as mothers—and that's perfectly OK, according to a new study.
sentences.translation: 父亲使用婴儿语的频率不如母亲，也不以同样的方式使用——根据一项新研究，这完全没问题。

structure.text: Fathers don't use baby talk as often or in the same ways as mothers
as often or in the same ways as mothers
and that's perfectly OK
according to a new study

difficult_points: as often or in the same ways as mothers 是比较结构，比较父亲和母亲的 baby talk 使用方式。
破折号后的 that's perfectly OK 是评价，说明差异并不一定有问题。
according to a new study 说明评价依据来自新研究。

sentences.sentence: Mark Van Dam of Washington State University at Spokane and colleagues equipped parents with recording devices and speech-recognition software to study the way they interacted with their youngsters during a normal day.
sentences.translation: 华盛顿州立大学斯波坎分校的 Mark Van Dam 及其同事给父母配备了录音设备和语音识别软件，以研究他们在普通一天里与孩子互动的方式。

structure.text: Mark Van Dam of Washington State University at Spokane and colleagues
equipped parents with recording devices and speech-recognition software
to study the way they interacted with their youngsters during a normal day
they interacted with their youngsters during a normal day

difficult_points: 主语由 Mark Van Dam 和 colleagues 并列构成。
equipped parents with ... 表示给父母配备研究设备。
to study ... 是目的状语，说明配备设备的研究目的。
the way 后省略关系词，they interacted ... 修饰 the way。

sentences.sentence: 'We found that moms do exactly what you'd expect and what's been described many times over,' Van Dam explains.
sentences.translation: Van Dam 解释说：“我们发现，妈妈们做的正是你所预期的，也是已经被描述过很多次的事情。”

structure.text: We found
that moms do exactly what you'd expect and what's been described many times over
what you'd expect
what's been described many times over
Van Dam explains

difficult_points: that 引导 found 的宾语从句，说明研究发现。
what you'd expect 和 what's been described many times over 是并列宾语内容。
what's been described 是现在完成时被动语态，表示相关行为已多次被描述。
many times over 强调重复次数很多。

sentences.sentence: 'But we found that dads aren't doing the same thing.
sentences.translation: “但我们发现，爸爸们并没有做同样的事情。

structure.text: But we found
that dads aren't doing the same thing

difficult_points: But 引出与上一句妈妈行为相反的发现。
that 引导宾语从句，说明爸爸们的行为不同。
aren't doing 使用现在进行时，描述研究中观察到的行为模式。

sentences.sentence: Dads didn't raise their pitch or fundamental frequency when they talked to kids.'
sentences.translation: 爸爸们和孩子说话时，并没有提高音高或基频。”

structure.text: Dads
didn't raise their pitch or fundamental frequency
when they talked to kids

difficult_points: their pitch or fundamental frequency 是并列宾语，说明父亲没有提高声音参数。
when 引导时间状语从句，限定是在和孩子说话时。
closing quote 表明这是上一句 Van Dam 引语的延续。

sentences.sentence: Their role may be rooted in what is called the bridge hypothesis, which dates back to 1975.
sentences.translation: 他们的角色可能根源于所谓的桥梁假说，这一假说可追溯到 1975 年。

structure.text: Their role
may be rooted in what is called the bridge hypothesis
what is called the bridge hypothesis
which dates back to 1975

difficult_points: Their role 指父亲在儿童语言输入中的角色。
may be rooted in 表示可能根源于某种理论或机制。
what is called the bridge hypothesis 是名词性从句，作 in 的宾语。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明该假说的时间来源。

sentences.sentence: It suggests that fathers use less familial language to provide their children with a bridge to the kind of speech they'll hear in public.
sentences.translation: 该假说认为，父亲使用较少家庭化的语言，是为了给孩子提供一座通向他们将在公共场合听到的那类语言的桥梁。

structure.text: It suggests
that fathers use less familial language to provide their children with a bridge to the kind of speech they'll hear in public
to provide their children with a bridge
they'll hear in public

difficult_points: It 指 the bridge hypothesis。
that 引导宾语从句，说明假说内容。
to provide ... with ... 表示父亲使用较少家庭化语言的目的。
they'll hear in public 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 the kind of speech。

sentences.sentence: 'The idea is that a kid gets to practice a certain kind of speech with mom and another kind of speech with dad, so the kid then has a wider repertoire of kinds of speech to practice,' says Van Dam.
sentences.translation: Van Dam 说：“这个想法是，孩子可以和妈妈练习某一种语言方式，和爸爸练习另一种语言方式，因此孩子就拥有更广泛的可练习语言类型库。”

structure.text: The idea is
that a kid gets to practice a certain kind of speech with mom and another kind of speech with dad
so the kid then has a wider repertoire of kinds of speech to practice
says Van Dam

difficult_points: that 引导表语从句，解释 the idea 的内容。
a certain kind ... and another kind ... 是并列结构，比较母亲和父亲提供的不同语言练习。
so 引导结果分句，说明孩子因此拥有更宽的语言类型库。
to practice 修饰 kinds of speech，表示可供练习的语言类型。

sentences.sentence: Scientists from the University of Washington and the University of Connecticut collected thousands of 30-second conversations between parents and their babies, fitting 26 children with audio-recording vests that captured language and sound during a typical eight-hour day.
sentences.translation: 华盛顿大学和康涅狄格大学的科学家收集了数千段父母与婴儿之间 30 秒的对话，并给 26 名儿童穿上录音背心，记录一个典型八小时日程中的语言和声音。

structure.text: Scientists from the University of Washington and the University of Connecticut
collected thousands of 30-second conversations between parents and their babies
fitting 26 children with audio-recording vests
that captured language and sound during a typical eight-hour day

difficult_points: collected 是主句谓语，说明科学家收集对话数据。
fitting ... 是现在分词短语，补充说明研究方法。
that captured ... 修饰 audio-recording vests，说明录音背心记录的内容。
during a typical eight-hour day 限定记录发生的时间范围。

sentences.sentence: The study found that the more baby talk parents used, the more their youngsters began to babble.
sentences.translation: 研究发现，父母使用的婴儿语越多，他们的孩子就越开始咿呀学语。

structure.text: The study found
that the more baby talk parents used, the more their youngsters began to babble
the more baby talk parents used
the more their youngsters began to babble

difficult_points: the more ..., the more ... 是关联比较结构，表示一个量增加，另一个量也增加。
parents used 是省略关系词的结构，修饰 baby talk 的使用量。
began to babble 表示婴儿开始发出咿呀学语的声音。

sentences.sentence: And when researchers saw the same babies at age two, they found that frequent baby talk had dramatically boosted vocabulary, regardless of socioeconomic status.
sentences.translation: 而当研究人员在这些婴儿两岁时再次见到他们时，他们发现频繁的婴儿语显著提升了词汇量，不论其社会经济地位如何。

structure.text: when researchers saw the same babies at age two
they found
that frequent baby talk had dramatically boosted vocabulary
regardless of socioeconomic status

difficult_points: when 引导时间状语从句，说明研究人员在孩子两岁时观察结果。
had dramatically boosted 使用过去完成时，表示词汇量提升发生在两岁观察之前。
regardless of socioeconomic status 表示这种提升不受社会经济地位影响。

sentences.sentence: 'Those children who listened to a lot of baby talk were talking more than the babies that listened to more adult talk or standard speech,' says Nairán Ramirez-Esparza of the University of Connecticut.
sentences.translation: 康涅狄格大学的 Nairán Ramirez-Esparza 说：“那些听了大量婴儿语的孩子，比听更多成人谈话或标准语言的婴儿说得更多。”

structure.text: Those children who listened to a lot of baby talk
who listened to a lot of baby talk
were talking more than the babies that listened to more adult talk or standard speech
that listened to more adult talk or standard speech
says Nairán Ramirez-Esparza of the University of Connecticut

difficult_points: who listened ... 修饰 Those children，说明比较中的一组孩子。
more than the babies ... 是比较结构，比较说话量。
that listened ... 修饰 the babies，说明另一组孩子接触的是成人谈话或标准语。
says ... 是引语后的报道分句。

sentences.sentence: 'We also found that it really matters whether you use baby talk in a one-on-one context,' she adds.
sentences.translation: 她补充说：“我们还发现，你是否在一对一情境中使用婴儿语真的很重要。”

structure.text: We also found
that it really matters whether you use baby talk in a one-on-one context
whether you use baby talk in a one-on-one context
she adds

difficult_points: it really matters 是固定结构，表示某事非常重要。
whether 引导真正重要的内容，即是否在一对一语境中使用 baby talk。
one-on-one context 表示一对一互动情境。
she adds 表示这句话是前一位研究者的补充说明。

sentences.sentence: The more parents use baby talk one-on-one, the more babies babble, and the more they babble, the more words they produce later in life.'
sentences.translation: 父母越是一对一地使用婴儿语，婴儿就越会咿呀学语；他们咿呀学语越多，日后产生的词汇就越多。”

structure.text: The more parents use baby talk one-on-one
the more babies babble
the more they babble
the more words they produce later in life

difficult_points: 句中连续使用两个 the more ..., the more ... 结构，表示递进的相关关系。
one-on-one 修饰 use baby talk 的方式，即一对一使用。
they 指 babies，后半句说明咿呀学语和日后词汇产出的关系。
later in life 表示在以后的人生阶段。

sentences.sentence: Another study suggests that parents might want to pair their youngsters up so they can babble more with their own kind.
sentences.translation: 另一项研究表明，父母也许应该把孩子配成伙伴，让他们能更多地和同类一起咿呀学语。

structure.text: Another study suggests
that parents might want to pair their youngsters up
so they can babble more with their own kind

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，说明另一项研究提出的建议。
might want to 语气委婉，表示可能值得这样做。
so 引导目的或结果状语从句，说明把孩子配对的作用。
their own kind 指其他婴儿，而不是成年人。

sentences.sentence: Researchers from McGill University and Université du Québec à Montréal found that babies seem to like listening to each other rather than to adults—which may be why baby talk is such a universal tool among parents.
sentences.translation: 麦吉尔大学和蒙特利尔魁北克大学的研究人员发现，婴儿似乎喜欢听彼此的声音，而不是听成人的声音——这也许就是婴儿语在父母中如此普遍的原因。

structure.text: Researchers from McGill University and Université du Québec à Montréal
found
that babies seem to like listening to each other rather than to adults
which may be why baby talk is such a universal tool among parents

difficult_points: that 引导 found 的宾语从句，说明研究发现。
rather than to adults 表示偏好对象的对比：彼此而非成人。
破折号后的 which 引导非限制性定语从句，概括前面发现并解释 baby talk 普遍性的可能原因。
such a universal tool among parents 表示在父母中非常普遍的工具。

sentences.sentence: They played repeating vowel sounds made by a special synthesizing device that mimicked sounds made by either an adult woman or another baby.
sentences.translation: 他们播放由一种特殊合成设备发出的重复元音声音，该设备模仿成年女性或另一个婴儿发出的声音。

structure.text: They
played repeating vowel sounds
made by a special synthesizing device
that mimicked sounds made by either an adult woman or another baby
made by either an adult woman or another baby

difficult_points: They 指前一句的研究人员。
made by a special synthesizing device 后置修饰 vowel sounds，说明声音来源。
that mimicked ... 修饰 device，说明设备的功能。
either an adult woman or another baby 表示设备模拟两类声音来源。

sentences.sentence: This way, only the impact of the auditory cues was observed.
sentences.translation: 通过这种方式，被观察到的只有听觉线索的影响。

structure.text: This way
only the impact of the auditory cues
was observed

difficult_points: This way 指使用合成设备控制声音来源的实验方法。
only 限定观察对象，强调排除了其他因素。
was observed 是被动语态，表示研究中被记录的是听觉线索的影响。

sentences.sentence: The team then measured how long each type of sound held the infants' attention.
sentences.translation: 随后，该团队测量每种声音吸引婴儿注意力的时间有多长。

structure.text: The team then measured
how long each type of sound held the infants' attention
each type of sound
held the infants' attention

difficult_points: then 表示实验步骤的先后顺序。
how long 引导名词性从句，作 measured 的宾语。
held the infants' attention 表示声音吸引并保持婴儿注意。

sentences.sentence: They found that the 'infant' sounds held babies' attention nearly 40 percent longer.
sentences.translation: 他们发现，“婴儿”声音吸引婴儿注意力的时间长了近 40%。

structure.text: They found
that the 'infant' sounds held babies' attention nearly 40 percent longer
nearly 40 percent longer

difficult_points: the 'infant' sounds 指设备模拟的婴儿声音，引号表示实验类别。
held babies' attention 是固定搭配，表示吸引并保持注意力。
nearly 40 percent longer 是比较数量，说明持续时间更长。

sentences.sentence: The baby noises also induced more reactions in the listening infants, like smiling or lip moving, which approximates sound making.
sentences.translation: 这些婴儿声音还在听音的婴儿身上诱发了更多反应，比如微笑或嘴唇移动，这接近于发声。

structure.text: The baby noises
also induced more reactions in the listening infants
like smiling or lip moving
which approximates sound making

difficult_points: induced more reactions 表示声音引发了更多反应。
in the listening infants 指在听这些声音的婴儿身上。
like smiling or lip moving 举例说明反应类型。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，概括嘴唇移动等反应接近发声行为。

sentences.sentence: The team theorizes that this attraction to other infant sounds could help launch the learning process that leads to speech.
sentences.translation: 该团队推测，这种对其他婴儿声音的吸引可能有助于启动通向语言的学习过程。

structure.text: The team theorizes
that this attraction to other infant sounds could help launch the learning process that leads to speech
this attraction to other infant sounds
could help launch the learning process
that leads to speech

difficult_points: theorizes 表示提出理论推测，而不是已经完全证明。
that 引导宾语从句，说明团队的推测内容。
this attraction 指前文婴儿对其他婴儿声音的注意偏好。
that leads to speech 修饰 the learning process，说明这一过程最终通向语言能力。

sentences.sentence: 'It may be some property of the sound that is just drawing their attention,' says study co-author Linda Polka.
sentences.translation: 研究共同作者 Linda Polka 说：“可能是这种声音的某种属性正在吸引他们的注意。”

structure.text: It may be some property of the sound
that is just drawing their attention
says study co-author Linda Polka

difficult_points: It may be 表示一种不确定的解释。
that is just drawing their attention 修饰 some property of the sound，说明吸引注意的是声音属性。
their 指听音的婴儿。
study co-author Linda Polka 是报道分句的主语。

sentences.sentence: 'Or maybe they are really interested in that particular type of sound because they are starting to focus on their own ability to make sounds.
sentences.translation: “或者也许他们真的对那种特定类型的声音感兴趣，因为他们正开始关注自己发声的能力。

structure.text: Or maybe they are really interested in that particular type of sound
because they are starting to focus on their own ability to make sounds
to make sounds

difficult_points: Or maybe 引出另一种可能解释。
they 指听到婴儿声音的婴儿。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释他们感兴趣的原因。
their own ability to make sounds 表示他们自身发声的能力。

sentences.sentence: We are speculating here but it might catch their attention because they recognize it as a sound they could possibly make.'
sentences.translation: 我们在这里是在推测，但它可能会吸引他们的注意，因为他们把它识别为自己也许能够发出的声音。”

structure.text: We are speculating here
but it might catch their attention
because they recognize it as a sound they could possibly make
they could possibly make

difficult_points: We are speculating here 明确说明这只是推测，降低结论确定性。
but 引出这种推测下声音可能吸引注意的结果。
it 指前文提到的婴儿声音。
they could possibly make 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 a sound。

sentences.sentence: In a study published in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, a total of 57 babies from two slightly different age groups—seven months and eleven and a half months—were played a number of syllables from both their native language (English) and a non-native tongue (Spanish).
sentences.translation: 在一项发表于《美国国家科学院院刊》的研究中，来自两个年龄略有不同群体的共 57 名婴儿——7 个月和 11 个半月——被播放了若干来自其母语（英语）和非母语（西班牙语）的音节。

structure.text: In a study published in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
a total of 57 babies from two slightly different age groups
seven months and eleven and a half months
were played a number of syllables
from both their native language (English) and a non-native tongue (Spanish)

difficult_points: published in ... 是过去分词短语，修饰 study。
a total of 57 babies 是主语，强调样本总数。
两个破折号之间的 seven months and eleven and a half months 解释 two slightly different age groups。
were played 是被动语态，表示这些婴儿被播放音节。

sentences.sentence: The infants were placed in a brain-activation scanner that recorded activity in a brain region known to guide the motor movements that produce speech.
sentences.translation: 这些婴儿被放入脑激活扫描仪中，该扫描仪记录了一个脑区的活动，而这个脑区被认为会指导产生语言的运动动作。

structure.text: The infants
were placed in a brain-activation scanner
that recorded activity in a brain region
known to guide the motor movements
that produce speech

difficult_points: were placed 是被动语态，说明婴儿被安置进扫描仪。
that recorded ... 修饰 scanner，说明扫描仪记录的内容。
known to guide ... 是过去分词短语，修饰 a brain region。
that produce speech 修饰 motor movements，说明这些运动动作会产生语言。

sentences.sentence: The results suggest that listening to baby talk prompts infant brains to start practicing their language skills.
sentences.translation: 结果表明，听婴儿语会促使婴儿大脑开始练习其语言技能。

structure.text: The results suggest
that listening to baby talk prompts infant brains to start practicing their language skills
listening to baby talk
prompts infant brains to start practicing their language skills

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，说明研究结果提示的内容。
listening to baby talk 是动名词短语，在从句中作主语。
prompt ... to ... 表示促使某对象做某事。
start practicing 使用 start 后接动名词，表示开始练习语言技能。

sentences.sentence: 'Finding activation in motor areas of the brain when infants are simply listening is significant, because it means the baby brain is engaged in trying to talk back right from the start, and suggests that seven-month-olds' brains are already trying to figure out how to make the right movements that will produce words,' says co-author Patricia Kuhl.
sentences.translation: 共同作者 Patricia Kuhl 说：“当婴儿只是听的时候，在大脑运动区域发现激活是重要的，因为这意味着婴儿大脑从一开始就参与到尝试回应说话中，并表明 7 个月大婴儿的大脑已经在试图弄清如何做出会产生词语的正确动作。”

structure.text: Finding activation in motor areas of the brain when infants are simply listening
when infants are simply listening
is significant
because it means the baby brain is engaged in trying to talk back right from the start
and suggests that seven-month-olds' brains are already trying to figure out how to make the right movements that will produce words
that will produce words

difficult_points: Finding activation ... 是动名词短语作主语，表示“发现激活”这一事实。
when infants are simply listening 限定发现激活发生在婴儿仅仅听的时候。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释为什么这一发现重要；means 和 suggests 在逻辑上并列。
how to make the right movements 是“疑问词 + 不定式”结构，作 figure out 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Another interesting finding was that while the seven-month-olds responded to all speech sounds regardless of language, the brains of the older infants worked harder at the motor activations of non-native sounds compared to native sounds.
sentences.translation: 另一个有趣发现是，虽然 7 个月大的婴儿会对所有语言的语音作出反应，不论语言种类如何，但较大婴儿的大脑在处理非母语声音的运动激活时，比处理母语声音更费力。

structure.text: Another interesting finding
was

that while the seven-month-olds responded to all speech sounds regardless of language, the brains of the older infants worked harder at the motor activations of non-native sounds compared to native sounds
while the seven-month-olds responded to all speech sounds regardless of language
the brains of the older infants worked harder at the motor activations of non-native sounds compared to native sounds

difficult_points: that 引导表语从句，说明另一个发现的内容。
while 引导让步或对比状语从句，比较 7 个月婴儿与较大婴儿的反应。
regardless of language 表示不管语言种类。
compared to native sounds 修饰 non-native sounds 的运动激活，说明比较对象。

sentences.sentence: The study may have also uncovered a process by which babies recognize differences between their native language and other tongues.
sentences.translation: 这项研究也可能揭示了一个过程，婴儿正是通过这一过程识别自己的母语与其他语言之间的差异。

structure.text: The study
may have also uncovered a process
by which babies recognize differences between their native language and other tongues

difficult_points: may have uncovered 表示可能已经揭示，语气保留不确定性。
by which 是介词提前的定语从句，修饰 a process。
differences between their native language and other tongues 是 recognize 的宾语，说明识别的对象。
other tongues 指母语以外的其他语言。
